title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 7: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 17 + 38 - 19?
A rectangle has a length of 9 cm and a width of 4 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='60' y='10' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>9 cm</text><text x='112' y='38' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled 9 cm long and 4 cm wide.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 6:45 pm and runs for 1 hour and 50 minutes. When does it end?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='18' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='55' y2='48' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='2' fill='currentColor'/></svg>

questions.question_text: Look at the sequence 4, 9, 16, 25, ... What is the next term?
Among these numbers, which is closest to 500?
A jug holds 2 liters of juice. How many 250 mL cups can be filled from the jug?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M 15 20 L 15 65 Q 15 70 20 70 L 45 70 Q 50 70 50 65 L 50 20 Z' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M 50 30 Q 60 30 60 40 Q 60 50 50 50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='75' y='45' width='20' height='25' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='22' y='45' font-size='9' fill='currentColor'>2 L</text><text x='76' y='80' font-size='8' fill='currentColor'>250 mL</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A jug labeled 2 L next to a smaller 250 mL cup.

questions.question_text: How many right angles does the L-shape below contain at its corners?
questions.image_description: An L-shaped hexagon formed by right angles.
questions.solution: The L-shape has right angles at all 6 vertices.

questions.question_text: Mira has 3 coins of 50 cents and 4 coins of 20 cents. How much money does she have in total?
In a race, Sam finished before Tim, Tim finished before Uri, and Uri finished before Vic. Who finished last?
questions.solution: Sam finished first, then Tim, then Uri, then Vic. So Vic finished last.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest two-digit number that is a multiple of 7?
questions.solution: Multiples of 7: 7, 14, 21, ... The smallest two-digit multiple is 14.

questions.question_text: If 4x - 5 = 19, what is the value of x?
questions.solution: Add 5 to both sides: 4x = 24. Divide by 4: x = 6.

questions.question_text: A square has area 49 square cm. A smaller square has sides half as long. What is the area of the smaller square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='80' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two squares side by side; the smaller has half the side length of the larger.

questions.question_text: In a bag, the ratio of red to blue marbles is 3:5. If there are 24 marbles total, how many are red?
Peter has 3 different shirts and 4 different pairs of pants. How many distinct outfits can he wear?
Tiles are arranged so that row 1 has 1 tile, row 2 has 3 tiles, row 3 has 5 tiles, and so on. How many tiles are in row 8?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='60' y='5' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='46' y='22' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='60' y='22' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='74' y='22' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='32' y='39' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='46' y='39' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='60' y='39' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='74' y='39' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='88' y='39' width='14' height='14' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangular tile pattern showing rows of 1, 3, 5 tiles.

questions.question_text: Three friends, Kia, Leo, and Mia, finished a race in some order. Kia was not last. Mia was not first. Which of the following orders is NOT possible?
questions.solution: Option E has Kia last, violating 'Kia was not last'.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of the first five prime numbers?
A triangle has angles of 50 degrees and 60 degrees. What is the third angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,85 120,85 70,15' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='28' y='80' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>50</text><text x='105' y='80' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>60</text><text x='65' y='32' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle with two angles labeled 50 and 60 degrees; the third is unknown.

questions.question_text: A rope of 7.2 meters is cut into 8 equal pieces. How long is each piece, in centimeters?
A shop sells apples at 40 cents each. Buy 5 and get 1 free. What is the lowest price to get exactly 12 apples?
questions.solution: Buy 10 paid apples, get 2 free. Cost = 10 x 0.40 = 4.00.

questions.question_text: How many three-digit numbers can be formed using the digits 2, 4, and 6, each used exactly once?
A rectangle is cut into 4 smaller rectangles by one horizontal and one vertical line. Three smaller rectangles have areas 6, 10, and 15 as shown. What is the area of the fourth?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='15' width='110' height='70' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='15' x2='60' y2='85' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='45' x2='125' y2='45' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='32' y='33' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='85' y='33' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>10</text><text x='32' y='68' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>?</text><text x='85' y='68' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>15</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into four parts: top-left 6, top-right 10, bottom-right 15, bottom-left unknown.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 72. What is the largest of the three?

Four cards show 3, 8, E, K face up. Each card has a letter on one side and a number on the other. To test the rule 'If a card shows a vowel, it has an even number on the other side,' which cards must be flipped?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='15' width='30' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='25' y='40' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>3</text><rect x='50' y='15' width='30' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='65' y='40' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>8</text><rect x='90' y='15' width='30' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='105' y='40' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>E</text><rect x='130' y='15' width='30' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='145' y='40' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>K</text></svg>
questions.solution: Flip E (verify even) and 3 (verify not a vowel). 8 and K cannot disprove the rule.

questions.question_text: What is the greatest common divisor of 36 and 84?
A cube has edge length 5 cm. If each edge is increased by 1 cm, by how many cubic cm does the volume increase?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,70 60,70 60,30 20,30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='60,30 75,20 75,60 60,70' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='20,30 35,20 75,20 60,30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.question_text: A recipe uses flour and sugar in the ratio 5:2. If 14 cups of sugar are used, how many cups of flour are needed?
In a sequence, each term after the first two is the sum of the previous two. If the 4th term is 11 and the 5th term is 18, what is the 1st term?
A number is multiplied by 4, then 7 is added, and the result is 43. What was the original number?
In a round-robin tournament with 6 teams, each team plays every other team once. How many games are played in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='60' cy='20' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='95' cy='40' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='95' cy='80' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='60' cy='100' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='25' cy='80' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='25' cy='40' r='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='95' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='95' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='60' y2='100' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='25' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='25' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='40' x2='95' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='40' x2='60' y2='100' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='40' x2='25' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='40' x2='25' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='80' x2='60' y2='100' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='80' x2='25' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='95' y1='80' x2='25' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='100' x2='25' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='100' x2='25' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='80' x2='25' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Six circles arranged in a hexagon with all pairs connected.
